folio.label: 192r
folio.l: And eke it is ny day , I may nat dwelle
Shortly I seye , as for conclusiou~
That I shal han , of this auisioun
Aduäsitee , and I seye forthermoore
That I ne telle , of laxatyues no store
For they ben venemous , I woot it wel
I hem deffye , I loue úe~ neuäe a del
¢ Now late vs speke of myrthe , & sthynte al this
Madame Partelote , so haue I blis
Of oo thyngö , god hatú sent me , large grace
For whan I se the beaute , of ©oure face
ñe ben so scarlet reed , a boute ©oure eyen
It maketú al my drede , for to dyen
And whan I fele a nygút , ©oure softe syde
Al be it , that I may nat , on ©ow ryde
For æ oure perche , is maad so narwe allas
I am so ful of ioye , and of solas
That I deffye , bothe sweuene and dreem
And witú that word , he fley doun fro the beem
For it was day , and eke his hennes alle
And witú a chukke , he gan hem for to calle
For he had founde a corn , lay in the ©erd
Real he was , he was nomore a ferd
He fethered Pertelote , twenty tyme
And tradde hire eke , as ofte , er it was péme
He loketú as it were , a grym leoun
And on his toos , he rometú vp & doun
Hi~ deyned nat to sette , his feet to g¼unde
And chukketú , whan he hatú , a corn I founde
And to úi~ rennen than~e , hise wyues alle
Thus real , as a Pénce is , in his halle
Leue I this Chaunteclere , in his pasture
And after wol I telle , his auenture
¢ Whan æ the monthe , in whicú the world bygan
That higúte Marche , whan god first maked man
Was complet , and I passed were also
Syn Marcú bygan , thretty dayes & two
Bifel that Chauntecler , in al his pride
His wyues seuene , walkyngö úi~ beside
Caste vp his eyen , to the brigút sonne
That in the signe of Taurus , hadde ronne
Twenty degrees and oon , and somwhat more
He knew by kynde , and by noon oæä loore
